content: Curiosity-driven intrinsic reward signals (the core of cutting-edge 2024-2025 active learning agent memory systems) can be repurposed to resolve the open problem of 'agent identity drift' when long-lived agents accrete uncurated new memory over multi-task lifespans, by weighting memory retention for experiences that triggered high intrinsic curiosity rather than just task reward or recency. This creates self-aligned memory hierarchies that preserve an agent's core exploratory behavioral signature without manual pruning.
